danger_signals.visual_form: Faction happiness metrics and electoral support visualization
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates policy misalignment with voter groups; signals risk of election loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Problem queue/alert system showing petrol protests, homelessness, pollution
danger_signals.meaning: Active crises requiring player intervention; unresolved situations damage electoral viability
Increases voter cynicism and reduces re-election likelihood

danger_signals.visual_form: Financial metrics showing country debt and negative balance
danger_signals.meaning: Forces difficult policy choices; high taxes trigger tax evasion and voter backlash

danger_signals.visual_form: Cynicism meter tied to broken promises and failed policy outcomes
danger_signals.meaning: Reflects voter disengagement and trust erosion; reduces electoral support

safety_signals.visual_form: Individual faction happiness sliders/bars (religious, patriotic, parents, capitalists, socialists, liberals, conservatives)
safety_signals.meaning: Indicates successful policy alignment with voter groups; signals electoral security
Demonstrates government competence; maintains voter trust and cynicism suppression

safety_signals.visual_form: Financial metrics showing balanced state and debt reduction
safety_signals.meaning: Indicates fiscal stability; enables sustainable policy funding without tax evasion risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Cleared problem alerts (disease cured, pollution managed, homelessness addressed)
safety_signals.meaning: Demonstrates active governance; improves overall election prospects

safety_signals.visual_form: Capital meter earned through loyal minister performance
safety_signals.meaning: Enables policy changes; higher reserves signal stronger political position

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Policy indicators: religious education funding, moral/social policies
Policy indicators: defense spending, foreign policy nationalism, law enforcement
Policy indicators: education funding, childcare support, public safety
Policy indicators: corporate tax rates, business regulation, economy policies
Policy indicators: welfare spending, progressive taxation, public services
Policy indicators: drug legalization, civil liberties, progressive law policies
Policy indicators: law enforcement, traditional values, death penalty, restrictive regulations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Situation alert highlighting pollution as ongoing problem
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must address through transport/environment policies or face environmental faction backlash

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Deficit indicators and debt visualization in financial metrics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forces policy retrenchment; high taxation triggers tax evasion; capitalists withdraw support

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Election timeline approaching; promise dialog triggered requiring two commitments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must make policy pivots to gain faction support; broken promises increase cynicism post-election

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Event notification triggered by game state or policy effects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Automatic outcome resolution; player profits or suffers consequences; affects multiple faction happiness simultaneously

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cynicism meter display and declining faction happiness across multiple groups
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Election prospects decline; player must rebuild trust through promise fulfillment and successful policy outcomes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Situation queue showing petrol protests, homelessness, law-and-order issues stacked simultaneously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must prioritize resource allocation and political capital spending across competing policy domains

environmental_storytelling.example: Tax policy slider position and amount communicate fiscal burden state; progressive taxation levels communicate wealth distribution philosophy
Active petrol protests, homelessness, pollution indicators communicate societal stress points without requiring narrative text
Individual voter profiles (e.g., 'Poor Conservative Smoker who is a Patriot') show intersectionality of faction membership and create implicit political tension narratives
Unfulfilled election promises visually degrade government credibility; communicates gap between political rhetoric and governance capability
Minister performance affects political capital generation; visualizes how insider political relationships enable executive action
Disease cure events and dilemma resolutions (e.g., senior judge appointment) communicate moral/governance complexity without prescribing outcomes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Electoral support increases; policy alignment visible through happiness meter; willingness to overlook minor policy failures
Electoral support decreases; happiness meter drops; cynicism increases if multiple broken promises accumulate
Cannot be fully satisfied; game explicitly makes universal approval 'practically impossible'; visual representation shows split loyalty in aggregated faction metrics
Demands two policy promises; generates dilemma dialog; holds player accountable at next election
Cynicism metric increases; happiness declines across affected factions; re-election probability decreases
Remains in active problem queue; continues to pressure voter satisfaction across related factions (e.g., pollution affects parents/liberals)
Removed from queue; generates positive feedback in relevant faction happiness; demonstrates government competence
Generates political capital for policy changes; higher loyalty = more capital available

notes: Democracy (2005) communicates world-state primarily through UI metrics and policy systems rather than rich visual/environmental design. The game uses abstract faction happiness sliders, situation alerts, and numerical metrics to manifest political reality. Political communication is entirely systemic: factions are defined by policy response patterns, not visual identity. The core tension (impossible to please all voters due to intersectional membership) is communicated through game mechanics, not visuals. World-state changes trigger UI feedback (promise dialogs, event notifications, situation alerts) that drives player behavioral response. The game emphasizes political consequence feedback loops over aesthetic danger/safety signaling.
